title: noronhia ecoronulata
intro: Noronhia ecoronulata, also known as the 'black plum' or 'black ebony', is a plant species native to Madagascar, characterized by its dark gray bark and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of Noronhia ecoronulata include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its rich content of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The health benefits of consuming this fruit include its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce oxidative stress, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The herbal preparations of Noronhia ecoronulata include making a tea from its leaves and bark, or consuming the fruit raw, with its fruit pulp being used as a natural dye due to its high anthocyanin content.
